comments.text: Suppose I want to earn $100.00 from Nvidia stock.  It was trading at $200.00 on June 11, 2026 and it rose to $205.00 that same day.  I would have had to invest $4,000 to buy 20 shares to get $100 profit.  That's a lot of skin in the game for many day traders and the playing field isn't level.

Click bait.  The "real truth" about day trading is statistically grim for retail investors: 97% to 99% of day traders lose money over the long term. While social media often portrays it as a path to quick wealth, academic and industry studies consistently show that the vast majority of participants fail to even match the returns of a simple index fund.

I like how you explained the whole checklist idea with trend, structure, then waiting for the pattern instead of just jumping into trades. The part about only looking for entries after price comes back to that broken level makes a lot of sense with how I’ve been mapping structure lately using the first light beacon. It kind of lines up with reading order flow and who’s actually taking control in that zone. Also appreciate you showing a losing trade too, most videos skip that part.
